title: execute_ask_user_question is a single function with cyclomatic complexity exceeding 15
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/tools/ask_user.rs:14-130 (execute_ask_user_question)
## Violation
A single function performs argument extraction, schema validation across 7 distinct constraint families (existence of `questions`, count bounds, duplicate question texts, header presence/length, multiSelect type, options count bounds, duplicate labels, description presence, preview type), and normalization. Branch points include 4 `let-else`, 1 explicit `if`, 1 `for` over questions, 2 nested `for` over options, 4 `if !x.is_*` checks, and 3 `if let Some` arms — cyclomatic complexity exceeds the V(G) > 15 reject threshold. Rejection logic is interleaved with normalization rather than separated, which raises cognitive complexity.

Callers (mcp.rs, acp.rs, CLI) cannot validate questions independently of the JSON tool wrapper without reimplementing this validation.

## Mandated refactor
Extract:
- `parse_question(value: &Value) -> Result<Question, String>` returning a typed `Question` struct.
- `validate_question_set(qs: &[Question]) -> Result<(), String>` for cross-question uniqueness.
- `normalize_question(q: Question) -> Question` for the `multi_select` -> `multiSelect` rename.
- A `Question` newtype whose constructor enforces invariants at the type boundary.

The tool entry point becomes a ~10-line orchestrator over typed values.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
